title: Exposed port on MacOS only works inside of podman machine vm
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running `podman run -p 8080:8080` does not expose the port on localhost:8080

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine start`

2. `podman run -p 8080:8080 quay.io/avulaj/web-service-gin`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % curl localhost:8080
curl: (52) Empty reply from server
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
If I run the application outside of a container, I see the following:
```
alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % curl localhost:8080
"Hello"%
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I have already looked at https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13111 and it does not seem to be the same issue I am having.

Through random trial and error, I found that if I use the following run command, I get a response from the application ONLY from within the podman machine vm. This is shown below:
```
alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % podman run -d --net=host quay.io/avulaj/web-service-gin
WARNING: image platform ({amd64 linux  [] }) does not match the expected platform ({arm64 linux  [] })
baebd85c558462cb3784ec71452be59785d94de81c66f7f25423fe86931f8eba

alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % curl localhost:8080
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8080 after 8 ms: Connection refused

alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 36.20221030.2.3
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Mon Nov 14 16:53:17 2022 from 192.168.127.1
[core@localhost ~]$ curl localhost:8080
"Hello"
```

**Output of `podman version`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
MacOS 13.0

comments.body: Did you try loopback IPv4 value instead of localhost? Try `curl http://127.0.0.1:8080` from host machine.

That doesn't seem to have helped unfortunately:
```
alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % podman run -d -p 8080:8080 quay.io/avulaj/web-service-gin 
WARNING: image platform ({amd64 linux  [] }) does not match the expected platform ({arm64 linux  [] })
0de34e81f226e5f87c6261c823d20806df3f5995bf25d8bcd0668297f99139a6

alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % curl http://127.0.0.1:8080
curl: (52) Empty reply from server
```

```
~ % podman run -d --rm -p8080:8080 quay.io/avulaj/web-service-gin 
WARNING: image platform ({amd64 linux  [] }) does not match the expected platform ({arm64 linux  [] })
c3718c8408554c1a484e54650db21f3907403beb6411274845acb222c40486e7
~ % curl http://localhost:8080
"Hello"%    
```
Works for me on M1 mac. Do you have `qemu-user-static` installed in podman VM? Because your image arch doesn't match your host arch.
Like in this blogpost https://edofic.com/posts/2021-09-12-podman-m1-amd64/ (you don't need install custom podman code snippet part as you have one supported already)

I'm also on an M1 mac, so this is really strange. I tried installing qemu on my machine and running the commands you have shown above and still no luck.  The output is as follows:

```
alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % podman machine ssh
```

I'm not sure if this helps at all, I confirmed with another dev on my team that they see the same results as you are, so I'm led to believe there's something weird going on with my local environment.  I'm going to try and recreate my podman machine itself and see if that helps.

I'm not quite sure exactly why, but rebuilding my machine has fixed the issue. In case it may give someone else more insight, here are the steps I took:
```
alexvulaj@avulaj-mac web-service-gin % podman machine rm && podman machine init -v $HOME:$HOME -v /private:/private

The following files will be deleted:

/Users/alexvulaj/.ssh/podman-machine-default
/Users/alexvulaj/.ssh/podman-machine-default.pub

/Users/alexvulaj/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock
/Users/alexvulaj/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_ovmf_vars.fd
/Users/alexvulaj/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json

        podman machine start
```
